action: Tap on the Google search bar at the bottom of the screen to activate the search interface.
changes: The screen transitions from the home screen to the Google search interface, displaying a search bar with the placeholder text "Search apps, web, and more..." and a microphone icon for voice search. A notification about location usage for search and Discover appears at the bottom of the screen.

action: Tap on the "X" icon to close the shipping destination popup.
changes: The shipping destination popup has been closed, revealing the underlying page content. The cookie consent banner is now visible at the bottom of the screen, offering options to accept or decline cookies. The page content, including the Black Friday announcement and product configurations, remains unchanged.

action: Tap on the search bar to activate it for entering a search query.
changes: After the tap action, the search bar becomes active, and the screen transitions to a search interface. The search bar is now focused, the keyboard appears at the bottom of the screen, and the "Recently searched" section is visible below the search bar. The main content of the page is replaced with the search interface, indicating that the user is now ready to input a search term.

action: Tap on the Chrome app icon to open the browser.
changes: The screen changes from the home screen to the Amazon website loaded in the Chrome browser. The browser interface, including the address bar and navigation controls, is visible at the top of the screen. The Amazon webpage content, such as the search bar, navigation menu, and promotional banners, is displayed below the browser controls.

action: Tap the back arrow at the top left corner of the screen to exit the settings menu and return to the main browser view.
changes: The screen transitions from the Chrome settings menu to the main browser view, displaying a Google search results page for the weather in Mountain View, CA. The settings menu is no longer visible, and the browser interface, including the address bar and search results, is now shown.

action: Tap on the "Notifications on lockscreen" option to open the notification settings for the lock screen.
changes: After the tap, a dialog box appears overlaying the current screen. This dialog is titled "Notifications on lockscreen" and presents three radio button options: "Show alerting and silent notifications" (selected), "Show alerting notifications only", and "Don't show notifications". The background of the main notifications settings screen is dimmed, indicating the dialog is the active element.

action: Swipe up on the product list to scroll down and view more items.
changes: The screen has scrolled down, revealing additional product listings below the initially visible items. The Alienware x15 R1 VR Ready Gaming Laptop is now fully visible, and a new advertisement for the MSI Pulse Gaming Laptop has appeared at the bottom of the screen. The previously visible portion of the page has moved upwards.

action: Tap the 'X' icon in the search bar to clear the current search query.
changes: The search query "gmail" is cleared from the search bar, and the search results for Gmail are replaced with a list of search suggestions related to "gmail". The screen now shows a list of suggested search terms instead of the Gmail app page.

action: Input text "amazon" in the search bar to search for amazon.com.
changes: After the action, the search bar now displays "amazon.com" and the page shows search results related to Amazon, including links to the Amazon website and specific product pages. The recent searches list is replaced by the search results.

action: Tap the plus icon to open a new browser tab.
changes: After the action, a new tab is opened in the browser, displaying the Best Buy website with a search for "dell xps". The previous page showing Costco products is now visible in a minimized tab below, indicating a switch to a different website.

action: Tap the "Add to Cart" button for the PNY - PRO Elite 512GB USB 3.1 Flash Drive to add it to the shopping cart.
changes: After the tap, a confirmation message "Added to cart" appears at the top of the screen with a checkmark next to the PNY flash drive. The "Add to Cart" button for that item changes to a "Compare" button. The screen now shows a "Continue shopping" button and a "Go to cart" button at the top, and the cart subtotal is updated to $74.99, indicating the item has been successfully added.

action: Tap on the shopping cart icon to view the items in the cart.
changes: After the tap, a context menu appeared over the Amazon webpage, displaying options related to the shopping cart icon, including "Open in new tab," "Open in incognito tab," "Copy link address," "Copy link text," "Download link," and "Share link." The main page content is dimmed behind this menu, indicating that the action successfully triggered a menu related to the cart.

action: Tap on the URL bar to select it for editing or entering a new web address.
changes: After the action, the browser's URL bar is selected and the webpage content is replaced with the new tab page, showing a search bar at the top and recent browsing history below. The previous flight search results are no longer visible.

action: Press the home button to return to the home screen.
changes: The screen changed from the "Set up email" screen within the Gmail app to the home screen. The home screen displays the date, time, weather, a Google search bar, and a row of app icons at the bottom. The background is a pink and purple gradient.

action: Tap on the "My apps" option in the Google Play Store app to view the list of installed applications.
changes: No changes observed after the action. The screen remains on the home screen with the "My apps" pop-up still visible, indicating that the tap did not successfully open the app list.

action: Tap on the Chrome app icon to open the Chrome browser.
changes: The screen transitions from the app drawer to the Chrome browser's loading screen, indicating that the Chrome app has been successfully launched. The app drawer is no longer visible, and the Chrome logo is displayed in the center of the screen.

action: Swipe down from the top of the screen to open the notification panel.
changes: The swipe action successfully opened the notification panel, revealing quick settings toggles and notifications at the top of the screen. The home screen icons and background are no longer visible, replaced by the notification shade.

action: Swipe up from the bottom of the screen to reveal the app drawer.
changes: The screen changes from the home screen to the app drawer, displaying all available apps in a grid layout. The home screen elements like the date, weather, and dock icons are no longer visible, replaced by the app icons.

action: Tap on the "asus rog" suggestion in the search dropdown to execute the search query.
changes: After the tap action, the page transitions from the search input screen to the search results page for "asus rog" on Best Buy. action: Press the home button to return to the home screen from the current app.

action: Tap on the Google Play Store icon to open the app store.

action: Tap on the Chrome app icon to open the web browser.

action: Tap on the Chrome browser icon to open the browser.

action: Tap on the Chrome app icon to open the Chrome browser.

action: Swipe up from the bottom of the screen to open the app drawer.

action: Tap on the 'X' icon in the search bar to clear the current search query.

action: Tap on the "Facebook Lite" search result to view the app details page.
changes: After the tap, the screen transitions from a search results list to the detailed app page for "Facebook Lite" on the app store. action: Input text "walmart" in the search bar to navigate to the Walmart website.
changes: After the action, the text "walmart" is entered into the search bar, replacing the previous text. action: Tap on the "Pop-ups and redirects" option in the site settings to access the pop-up management settings.

The title and description of the setting are clearly visible, and the navigation bar at the top includes a back arrow and a menu icon.

action: Swipe up from the bottom of the screen to scroll up and view more product details.

The image of the Bose speaker is now fully visible.

action: Tap the "Add to Cart" button to add the Razer Thresher headset to the shopping cart.

action: Tap on the Chrome browser icon to open the web browser.

action: Tap on the product image of the LG 27GP700-B.AUS 27" UltraGear Nano IPS monitor to view its details.

action: Press the home button to return to the home screen from the Google Photos settings.

action: Tap on the date "6" in the calendar to view the events scheduled for that day.
